candidate_summary: K-voice orthogonal LoRA channel adapter inspired by Lithuanian sutartinės secondal-dissonance polyphony: decomposes ΔW into K voice-channels with Stiefel-orthonormality, regularizes subspace-distance > τ_dissonance between voices, and uses a shared rhythmic mask M_rhythm to gate time-slices across voices.
content_words: K-voice orthogonal LoRA channel separation Stiefel

results.title: Ortho-LoRA Multi-Task Gradient Orthogonal
results.rationale: Projects conflicting gradients onto orthogonal complement in LoRA subspace; shares orthogonality but lacks K-voice channel decomposition, subspace-distance dissonance regularization, and rhythmic mask gating.

results.title: Learn Preserve Diversify Orthogonal LoRA Group
results.rationale: Group of LoRA modules with orthogonal regularization closely parallels K-voice channels with orthogonality; kw>=2 forces hit. Lacks Stiefel manifold, subspace-distance dissonance threshold, and rhythmic interlock mask.

results.title: O-LoRA Continual Orthogonal Subspace
results.rationale: Continual LoRA orthogonal subspaces target sequential tasks, not simultaneous K-voice independence with rhythmic gating; no Stiefel constraint or time-slice mask.

results.title: LoRA Interference Orthogonal Subspaces Merging
results.rationale: Orthogonal LoRA subspaces for model merging; lacks voice-channel/Stiefel/dissonance subspace-distance/rhythmic mask mechanisms.

results.title: OLieRA Lie Group Orthogonal Continual LoRA
results.rationale: Lie group orthogonality across continual tasks; structurally similar manifold flavor but no concurrent K-voice channels, dissonance distance threshold, or rhythmic interlock.

results.title: CLoRA Controlled Null-Space Direction
results.rationale: Null-space direction constraint via orthogonal regularization overlaps with null-space-traversal form; kw>=2 forces hit. Differs by lacking K-voice channelization, Stiefel-manifold, dissonance τ regularization, and rhythmic gating.

results.title: StelLA Stiefel Subspace LoRA
results.rationale: Stiefel-manifold orthonormality on LoRA decomposition directly matches the Stiefel-orthonormality component of the candidate; sem>=0.7 and kw>=2 force hit. Differs by single subspace (not K voice-channels), no subspace-distance dissonance threshold, no rhythmic interlock mask.

results.title: DevFD MoE-LoRA Real+Fake Orthogonal Subspaces
results.rationale: Developmental MoE-LoRA with orthogonal direction enforcement parallels multi-channel orthogonal decomposition; kw>=2 forces hit. Lacks Stiefel manifold, dissonance-distance threshold, and rhythmic time-slice mask.

decision_rationale: Four prior-art results trigger the hit rule. LPD, CLoRA, StelLA, and DevFD each achieve keyword_overlap_count>=2 by sharing core terms (orthogonal, LoRA, null-space, Stiefel, subspaces/channels). StelLA additionally crosses semantic_score>=0.7 because Stiefel-manifold orthonormality on LoRA is a near-direct match to the candidate's Stiefel-orthonormal K-voice decomposition. The candidate's distinguishing features (secondal-dissonance τ subspace-distance regularization and shared rhythmic interlock mask) are not enough to differentiate when the foundational mechanism (Stiefel-orthogonal multi-subspace LoRA) is already established prior art. Verdict: FAIL (novelty insufficient).
